intro: Pomatocalpa poilanei, also known as the Polynesian orchid, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Pomatocalpa poilanei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
